Functions for retrieving the MCU serial number and converting CPU cycles to milliseconds.

# Purpose
This header file provides two functions related to microcontroller (MCU) operations. The function `mcu_get_serial` returns a pointer to a 96-bit serial number that is programmed by the MCU manufacturer, which can be used for unique identification of the MCU. The function `mcu_cycles_to_milliseconds` converts a given number of CPU ticks, represented as a 64-bit integer, into milliseconds, facilitating time-related calculations based on the MCU's clock cycles. This code offers narrow functionality specific to MCU operations and is intended to be included in other C++ source files.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stdint.h`
